short_name: Burn Study- Tranexamic Acid Versus Thrombin in Split Thickness Skin Graft
title: Burn Study- Tranexamic Acid Versus Thrombin in Split Thickness Skin Graft

skipped_criteria: English or Spanish speaking: language is not a field in the schema.
Flame or scald burn injury (inclusion): burn sub-type/cause cannot be distinguished within the allowed 'burn' mechanism enum.
<=10% total body surface area (TBSA) partial thickness: TBSA percentage is not a field in the schema.
<=5% total body surface area (TBSA) deep partial or full thickness: TBSA percentage and burn depth are not fields in the schema.
Skin graft harvested at 1/12 inch depth, meshed 1:1 or pie crusted: surgical/procedural detail is not a field in the schema.
>10% total body surface area (TBSA) partial thickness burn (exclusion): TBSA percentage is not a field in the schema.
>5% total body surface area (TBSA) surgical area of burn injury (exclusion): TBSA percentage and burn depth are not fields in the schema.

Traumatic or chemical burn mechanism (exclusion): burn sub-type cannot be distinguished within the allowed mechanism enum; 'burn' enum value does not differentiate flame/scald from traumatic/chemical.
Patients on immunosuppression: medication/treatment history is not a field in the schema.
Uncontrolled diabetes: medical history/lab values are not fields in the schema.
Known diagnosis of peripheral vascular disease or diagnosis at time of injury: medical history is not a field in the schema.
Patients with acquired defective color vision: medical history is not a field in the schema.

Patients with subarachnoid hemorrhage: subarachnoid hemorrhage is a specific subtype not directly mappable to presumed_intracranial_hemorrhage without ambiguity; partially encoded as presumed_intracranial_hemorrhage exclusion.
Patients with active intravascular clotting or known hypersensitivity reactions to tranexamic acid: coagulation status and drug hypersensitivity are not fields in the schema.
Inability to give informed consent: consent capacity is not a field in the schema.
